agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A feature request to integrate Warehouse Management (stock) with Sales/Order Management (orders) by blocking orders when stock is insufficient and providing real-time stock visibility to the sales team. This fundamentally changes the relationship between two currently decoupled bounded contexts.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration between Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management. The request implies coupling that violates the current context boundaries where orders are placed independently of stock levels.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"StockLevel","definition":"An integer (>= 0) in the Warehouse Management context representing the current quantity of a Product on hand. Owned exclusively by Context A."},{"term":"Order","definition":"A sales-side aggregate in Context B representing a customer's intent to purchase one or more products."},{"term":"OrderLine","definition":"A line item within an Order, referencing a Product by ID and specifying a Quantity requested."},{"term":"Product","definition":"An entity that exists in both contexts but with different meanings — in Context A it is an inventory-managed item with a StockLevel; in Context B it is a catalog reference on an OrderLine."},{"term":"Stock Reservation","definition":"A domain concept currently MISSING from both contexts — a temporary hold on stock allocated to a pending order, distinct from both available stock and picked stock."},{"term":"Available Stock","definition":"A derived concept: StockLevel minus all outstanding reservations. This does NOT exist yet and must be introduced to support the feature correctly."},{"term":"Stock Available Indicator","definition":"A read-side projection of Available Stock exposed to the Sales context. Must be eventually consistent, not a live query into Warehouse internals."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"StockLevel in the Warehouse context cannot go below zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a physical constraint — you cannot ship items you do not have. Violating this corrupts inventory state.","testable_assertion":"After any operation that decreases StockLevel, assert StockLevel >= 0."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An Order placement in Context B must NOT directly read or write StockLevel in Context A. Context boundaries must be preserved.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Direct coupling between bounded contexts creates a distributed data integrity problem and violates DDD context autonomy. If Sales directly queries or locks Warehouse state, both contexts lose independent deployability and a failure in one cascades to the other.","testable_assertion":"No Order placement code path contains a synchronous call into Warehouse Management internals."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Stock sufficiency checks must operate on Available Stock (StockLevel minus outstanding reservations), not raw StockLevel.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Using raw StockLevel without accounting for reservations means two concurrent orders could both 'see' stock and both be accepted, leading to oversell and a BR-1 violation at pick time.","testable_assertion":"Given StockLevel=10 and existing reservations totaling 8, a new order for quantity 3 is rejected (available=2 < 3)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Reservations must have a time-to-live (TTL) and expire if the order is not confirmed/picked within the window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The TTL duration is a business policy decision (e.g., 15 minutes, 1 hour, 24 hours). A VP could change this threshold without corrupting the system.","testable_assertion":"A reservation created at T that is not confirmed by T+TTL is automatically released, increasing Available Stock by the reserved quantity."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The 'stock available' indicator shown to the Sales team must be eventually consistent, not guaranteed real-time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Real-time consistency across bounded contexts requires synchronous coupling. Eventually consistent is a policy choice that preserves autonomy. The staleness window is tunable.","testable_assertion":"The stock indicator reflects a state no older than the configured staleness threshold (e.g., 5 seconds)."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"An order blocked for insufficient stock must transition to a distinct state (e.g., 'Rejected-InsufficientStock') rather than silently failing or remaining in a generic 'Failed' state.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a UX and operational policy — the sales team needs to distinguish stock rejections from other failures. But the specific state name is a policy choice.","testable_assertion":"When an order is blocked due to insufficient stock, its status is 'Rejected-InsufficientStock' and the reason includes which product(s) were insufficient."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A product with StockLevel=10 and zero existing reservations","when":"A customer places an order for quantity 5 of that product","then":"The order is accepted, a reservation of 5 is created in the Warehouse context, and Available Stock is now 5"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A product with StockLevel=10 and existing reservations totaling 8","when":"A customer places an order for quantity 3 of that product","then":"The order is blocked with status 'Rejected-InsufficientStock' because Available Stock is 2, which is less than 3"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A product with StockLevel=10 and a reservation of 5 that has exceeded its TTL","when":"The reservation expiry process runs","then":"The reservation is released and Available Stock returns to 10"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"Two customers simultaneously place orders for quantity 6 each on a product with StockLevel=10 and zero reservations","when":"Both orders are processed concurrently","then":"Exactly one order is accepted and one is rejected. StockLevel never goes below 0. No oversell occurs."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"The Warehouse context publishes a stock-changed event","when":"The Sales context consumes the event","then":"The 'stock available' indicator updates within the configured staleness window without the Sales context querying Warehouse internals directly"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"The Warehouse context is temporarily unavailable","when":"A customer places an order","then":"The order is NOT silently accepted as if stock were unlimited. The system either queues the order for later validation or rejects it with a clear 'stock check unavailable' status."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Multiple OrderLines in a single Order reference the same Product","expected_behavior":"Quantities must be aggregated before the stock check. An order with two lines of 5 each for Product X must check against Available Stock of 10, not 5."},{"scenario":"Supplier receipt (StockLevel increase) arrives between order placement and reservation creation","expected_behavior":"The reservation should use the most current Available Stock. The incoming supply should not be lost or double-counted."},{"scenario":"Order is cancelled after reservation is created but before picking","expected_behavior":"The reservation must be released, returning the quantity to Available Stock. Failure to release creates phantom reservations that permanently reduce sellable stock."},{"scenario":"StockLevel is exactly 0","expected_behavior":"All orders for that product are blocked. The indicator shows 'unavailable'. No reservation with quantity 0 is created."},{"scenario":"Product exists in Sales catalog but not yet created in Warehouse context","expected_behavior":"The stock check must fail gracefully — the order should be blocked with a clear reason ('product not in warehouse system'), not crash or default to unlimited stock."},{"scenario":"Network partition between contexts during order placement","expected_behavior":"Must not default to 'stock available'. Fail closed (reject or queue), not open."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Blocking orders on insufficient stock eliminates oversell costs (refunds, expedited shipping, customer compensation). However, stale stock indicators or overly aggressive blocking could cause lost sales on items that are actually available. The reservation TTL policy directly impacts revenue — too short loses legitimate orders, too long holds phantom stock.","operational":"Introduces operational coupling between Warehouse and Sales teams. Warehouse outages now directly block sales. Reservation management adds a new operational burden (monitoring expiry, handling edge cases). The sales team gains visibility but also gains a new failure mode to manage.","user":"Customers get immediate feedback instead of post-purchase cancellation emails — significantly better experience. Sales team gets stock visibility they currently lack. However, false negatives (blocking orders when stock exists) will frustrate both customers and sales staff.","risk":"HIGH: This integration creates a distributed consistency problem. The primary risks are: (1) oversell from race conditions if reservations are not implemented correctly, (2) undersell from stale data or unreleased reservations, (3) cascading failures if Warehouse unavailability blocks all order placement, (4) eventual consistency lag making the 'real-time' indicator misleading to sales staff who treat it as authoritative.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Better experience when stock exists (immediate confirmation). Worse experience if stock indicator is stale and order gets rejected after appearing available."},{"group":"Sales Team","impact":"Gains stock visibility but loses the ability to place orders freely. Must understand that the indicator is eventually consistent, not a guarantee."},{"group":"Warehouse Team","impact":"Now responsible for a system whose availability directly gates revenue. Reservation management adds operational complexity."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Fewer oversell refunds. But reservations create a new concept of 'committed but not yet picked' stock that may need accounting treatment."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must implement a cross-context integration pattern (e.g., domain events + anti-corruption layer) rather than direct coupling. This is architecturally significant."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The feature request contains a fundamental contradiction: it asks for real-time stock checking across bounded contexts that were explicitly designed to be decoupled ('Orders are placed by customers without checking Context A'). The request also says 'automatically block' which implies synchronous coupling, while 'real-time indicator' implies an async read model. These are architecturally incompatible without introducing new domain concepts (reservations, anti-corruption layer) that the spec does not mention. The spec is also silent on critical policies: reservation TTL, staleness tolerance, failure mode when Warehouse is unavailable.","high_confidence_areas":["StockLevel >= 0 invariant","Need for a reservation concept to prevent oversell","Context boundaries must not be violated by direct coupling","Eventually consistent indicator is the correct pattern"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'automatically block' means synchronous rejection or async saga","Reservation TTL policy","Acceptable staleness for the stock indicator","Failure behavior when Warehouse context is unreachable","Whether partial order fulfillment is acceptable (some lines sufficient, others not)"],"assumptions":["A Reservation concept must be introduced in the Warehouse context — the spec does not mention this but it is required for correctness","The integration should use domain events and an anti-corruption layer, not direct database queries","The stock indicator is a read-model projection, not a live query","Orders are all-or-nothing (if any line has insufficient stock, the entire order is blocked)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The spec states orders are 'placed without checking Context A' (current design), but the feature requests synchronous stock blocking at order placement time. This fundamentally changes the contract of Context B. Additionally, 'automatically block' (synchronous) and 'real-time indicator' (async read model) suggest two different integration patterns that need to be reconciled.","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Sales Team","Customers"],"options":["A) Synchronous stock check: Order placement calls Warehouse synchronously. Simple but creates tight coupling and Warehouse becomes a single point of failure for sales.","B) Saga/choreography: Order is tentatively accepted, then asynchronously validated against stock. Better decoupling but the customer experience includes a delay before confirmation.","C) CQRS read model: Stock indicator is a local read model in Context B (async projection). Order placement checks THIS local model, not Context A directly. Best balance but introduces eventual consistency risk."],"recommendation":"Option C — CQRS read model with reservations. The stock indicator and the order-time check both use a local projection in Context B, maintained via domain events from Context A. A reservation request is sent to Context A asynchronously. This preserves context boundaries while enabling the feature. But the business must accept that 'real-time' means 'near-real-time' (seconds, not milliseconds)."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"The spec does not define: (1) Reservation TTL duration, (2) acceptable staleness for the stock indicator, (3) behavior when Warehouse context is unavailable, (4) whether partial fulfillment is allowed (some lines have stock, others don't), (5) whether backorders are a concept. All of these are critical business policy decisions.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Warehouse Team","Product Management"],"options":["Proceed with sensible defaults and make them configurable","Block implementation until all policies are defined"],"recommendation":"Define minimum viable policies before implementation: reservation TTL (suggest 30 minutes), staleness threshold (suggest 5 seconds), failure mode (suggest fail-closed / reject), partial fulfillment (suggest reject entire order). Make all configurable."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Any implementation that has Context B directly querying or writing to Context A's database or internal APIs without an anti-corruption layer","business_rule_broken":"BR-2 — Context boundary preservation"},{"violation":"Any implementation that checks raw StockLevel without accounting for existing reservations","business_rule_broken":"BR-3 — Available Stock must account for reservations, otherwise concurrent orders will oversell"},{"violation":"Any implementation that silently accepts orders when stock status is unknown (Warehouse unavailable)","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 — StockLevel cannot go below 0; accepting orders blindly risks violating this invariant at pick time"}]}
